perturbation.original_text: In consideration of the services to be rendered under this Agreement as set forth above, the Company shall be entitled to compensation in the amount of $150,000.00 (One Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars), to be paid in equal installments of $37,500.00 (Thirty Seven Thousand Five Hundred Dollars) on January 1, 2008, April 1, 2008, July 1, 2008 and October 1, 2008. If Cano fails to make any payment when required by this Agreement, the Company shall have the option of canceling this Agreement following the passage of ten (10) days after having given Cano written notice of its default. While the Company shall have no further obligation to Cano following cancellation of the Agreement, Cano shall remain liable for all unpaid installments for the then current term of the Agreement, which installments shall be immediately due and payable.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of the services to be rendered under this Agreement as set forth above, the Company shall be entitled to compensation in the amount of $150,000.00 (One Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars), to be paid in equal installments of $37,500.00 (Thirty Seven Thousand Five Hundred Dollars) on January 1, 2008, April 1, 2008, July 1, 2008 and October 1, 2008. If Cano fails to make any payment when required by this Agreement, the Company shall have the option of canceling this Agreement following the passage of ten (10) days after having given Cano written notice of its default. If Cano makes the payment within those ten (10) days after notification, this agreement stays in effect, without future late penalties. While the Company shall have no further obligation to Cano following cancellation of the Agreement, Cano shall remain liable for all unpaid installments for the then current term of the Agreement, which installments shall be immediately due and payable.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. The original text states Cano remains liable for all unpaid installments if the agreement is cancelled. The addition says if Cano pays in ten days after notice, the agreement stays in effect without future late penalties. This contradicts the 'immediately due and payable' clause if Cano pays within the ten-day grace period, creating uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Payment of Money. In consideration of the services to be rendered under this Agreement as set forth above, the Company shall be entitled to compensation in the amount of $150,000.00 (One Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars), to be paid in equal installments of $37,500.00 (Thirty Seven Thousand Five Hundred Dollars) on January 1, 2008, April 1, 2008, July 1, 2008 and October 1, 2008. If Cano fails to make any payment when required by this Agreement, the Company shall have the option of canceling this Agreement following the passage of ten (10) days after having given Cano written notice of its default. If Cano makes the payment within those ten (10) days after notification, this agreement stays in effect, without future late penalties. While the Company shall have no further obligation to Cano following cancellation of the Agreement, Cano shall remain liable for all unpaid installments for the then current term of the Agreement, which installments shall be immediately due and payable.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text states Cano remains liable for all unpaid installments if the agreement is cancelled. The added sentence stating if Cano pays within ten days, the agreement stays in effect without future late penalties, directly contradicts the clause that all unpaid installments become 'immediately due and payable'. This creates uncertainty and inconsistency regarding the payment terms upon default, thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Any notice provided for under the terms of this Agreement by either party to the other shall be in writing and may be effected by personal delivery in writing or registered or certified mail, return receipt requested. Notice to Boyd shall be sufficient if made or addressed to 2003 Navasota Cove, Westlake, Texas 76092. Notice to Cano shall be sufficient if made or addressed to Cano Petroleum, Inc., 801 Cherry Street, Suite 3200, Fort Worth, Texas 76102. Each party may change the address at which notice may be sent to that party by giving notice of such change to the other party in accordance with the provisions of this Paragraph.
perturbation.changed_text: Any notice provided for under the terms of this Agreement by either party to the other shall be in writing and must be effected by electronic mail with confirmation receipt. Notice to Boyd shall be sufficient if made or addressed to legal@boydenterprises.com. Notice to Cano shall be sufficient if made or addressed to legal@canopetroleum.com. Each party may change the email address at which notice may be sent to that party by giving notice of such change to the other party in accordance with the provisions of this Paragraph.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows notice by 'personal delivery in writing or registered or certified mail'. The changed text mandates 'electronic mail'. This in-text contradiction creates confusion on acceptable ways to provide notice, creating uncertainty on what would be valid and enforcable notice.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notice. Any notice provided for under the terms of this Agreement by either party to the other shall be in writing and must be effected by electronic mail with confirmation receipt. Notice to Boyd shall be sufficient if made or addressed to legal@boydenterprises.com. Notice to Cano shall be sufficient if made or addressed to legal@canopetroleum.com. Each party may change the email address at which notice may be sent to that party by giving notice of such change to the other party in accordance with the provisions of this Paragraph.
perturbation.justification: The changed text mandates notice by electronic mail, whereas the contradicted text states the same. However, The original text allows notice by 'personal delivery in writing or registered or certified mail, return receipt requested.' This creates an inconsistency regarding acceptable ways to provide notice, making it unclear whether notice by mail is also valid.
